articles.title: Microsoft 365 Android Apps Let Any App Steal Account Tokens via Leftover Debug Flag
articles.summary: A development flag left switched on in production builds of several Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled the check that limits account-token sharing to trusted Microsoft apps. Any other app on the same phone could ask for the signed-in user's token and get it, then read email, open files, browse the calendar, and send messages as that user. No password, no login screen, no permission prompt.

keypoints: Debug flag exposed Microsoft 365 Android token authentication bypass
Malicious apps could steal credentials without user consent notifications

analysis.title: Technical Vulnerability Details
analysis.desc: Development debug flag left enabled in production disables token-sharing validation checks across Microsoft 365 Android apps.

analysis.title: Security Impact and Risk
analysis.desc: Attackers gain unauthorized access to email, calendar, files, messaging without credentials, passwords, or permission prompts.

analysis.title: Remediation and Prevention
analysis.desc: Microsoft must release patched builds removing debug flags and implement token validation safeguards in production deployments.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Microsoft 365 Android Debug Flag Exposes User Account Tokens
